title: Who Really Pays for the Remote Work Revolution?
subtitle: 远程办公成本转嫁，谁在买单

paragraphs.en: As many office workers adapt to remote work, cities may undergo fundamental change if offices remain underutilized. Who will benefit if working from home becomes the norm? Employers argue they make considerable savings on real estate when workers shift from office to home work.
paragraphs.zh: 随着大量上班族适应远程办公，若办公室持续空置，城市或将经历根本性变革。若居家办公成为常态，谁会是真正的受益者？雇主们声称，员工从办公室转向家中工作时，他们可大幅节省房地产开支。

paragraphs.en: However, these savings result from passing costs on to workers. Unless employees are fully compensated, this could become a variant of parasitic capitalism, whereby corporate profits increasingly rely on extracting value from the public—and now personal—realm, rather than on generating new value.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，这些节省实则源于将成本转嫁给员工。除非员工得到充分补偿，否则这可能演变为寄生资本主义的一种变体，企业利润愈发依赖从公共领域乃至个人领域榨取价值，而非创造新增价值。

words.meaning: adj. 寄生的，依赖他人的

paragraphs.en: Though employers are backed by a chorus of remote work advocates, others note the loneliness, reduced productivity and inefficiencies of extended remote work. If working from home becomes permanent, employees will have to dedicate part of their private space to work. This requires purchasing desks, chairs and office equipment.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管雇主们有一众远程办公倡导者摇旗呐喊，但也有人指出长期远程办公带来的孤独感、效率下降与种种低效问题。倘若居家办公成为常态，员工将不得不划出部分私人空间用于工作，还需购置桌椅及办公设备。

paragraphs.en: It also means having private space dedicated to work: the space must be heated, cleaned, maintained and paid for. That depends on many things, but for purposes of illustration, I have run some estimates for Montreal.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，专门划出的工作空间还需供暖、清扫、维护并承担相应费用。尽管具体情形因人而异，为便于说明，我以蒙特利尔为例做了一番估算。

paragraphs.en: The exercise is simple but important, since it brings these costs out of the realm of speculation into the realm of meaningful discussion. Rough calculations show that the savings made by employers when their staff works from home are of similar value to the compensation workers should receive for setting up offices at home. What does this mean for offices in cities?
paragraphs.zh: 此项测算虽简单却意义重大，因为它将这些成本从猜测领域拉入了有意义的讨论范畴。粗略计算表明，雇主因员工居家办公而节省的开支，与员工在家设立办公室理应获得的补偿大致相当。这对城市中的办公楼而言意味着什么？

paragraphs.en: One of two things may happen: Employers pass these costs onto employees. This would be a form of expropriation, with employees absorbing production costs that have traditionally been paid by the employer. This represents a considerable transfer of value from employees to employers.
paragraphs.zh: 可能出现两种情况之一：雇主将这些成本转嫁给员工。此举无异于一种剥夺，员工被迫承担了本应由雇主支付的生产成本，意味着价值从员工向雇主的大规模转移。

paragraphs.en: When employees are properly compensated, employers' real estate savings will be modest. If savings are modest, then the many advantages of working in offices—such as lively atmosphere, rapidity of communication, team-building and acclimatization of new employees—will encourage employers to shelve the idea of remote work. They may, like Yahoo in 2013, encourage employees to work most of the time from corporate office space.
paragraphs.zh: 而若员工得到合理补偿，雇主在房地产上的节省便会十分有限。一旦节省幅度不大，办公室工作的诸多优势——如活跃的氛围、迅捷的沟通、团队建设及新员工融入——就会促使雇主搁置远程办公的念头。他们或许会像2013年的雅虎那样，鼓励员工多数时间回归公司办公。
